steps.title: Find the contrasting claim
steps.content: The phrase “in fact, such memories are often not explained by familiarity with erroneous versions” reveals a contrast: the researchers tested a hypothesis that their findings then contradicted. The blank should describe what they initially thought caused the false memories.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: ”Exposure to” incorrect renditions proposes a logical mechanism—repeated contact with erroneous versions could plausibly produce false memories. The other choices either don't make grammatical sense or don't describe plausible causes of false memory formation. That matches choice B — B is correct.
